analysis.priorityItem: Add full cloud lifecycle end-to-end integration tests
analysis.description: Build an end-to-end integration suite that exercises the unified cloud flow across commands: `hal cloud setup`, `hal run|auto|review --cloud`, then `status`, `logs`, `cancel`, and `pull`. Use an ephemeral store/runtime harness so tests validate real command interoperability, workflow-kind persistence, and artifact handling without production dependencies. Assert both human-readable and JSON outputs at key lifecycle checkpoints.
analysis.rationale: The highest remaining risk after the unified cloud rollout is cross-command regression that unit and command-local tests may miss. This work has high impact because it validates the primary user journey end-to-end and protects recent changes in shared flags, resolver precedence, workflow dispatch, and output contracts. It is clearly scoped in the report and is not a duplicate of the already-completed unified cloud UX PRD.
acceptanceCriteria: An automated integration suite runs `setup -> run --cloud -> status -> logs -> pull` against an ephemeral store and passes deterministically.
The suite includes `auto --cloud` and `review --cloud` lifecycle scenarios with persisted `workflowKind` assertions.
A cancel-path scenario validates `hal cloud cancel` updates workflow state to the expected terminal status.
Lifecycle tests assert both human and `--json` output contracts, including stable camelCase fields.
Secret-bearing values are redacted in all asserted outputs for cloud and auth-related responses.
Artifact retrieval assertions verify expected artifact-group metadata and pulled file presence.
The harness performs isolated setup/teardown and does not require external network services.
CI executes the lifecycle integration suite on pull requests and fails the pipeline on regressions.
